design_philosophy.core_loop: Conquer territories by recruiting armies, managing food/resources, defeating enemy captains, and tipping the balance of power scale. Control multiple armies through captain subordinates with command lag based on distance.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (food, equipment, captains) vs. territorial expansion; managing distributed armies with communication delays vs. coordinated conquest objectives.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a medieval conquest campaign where you build influence through tactical army deployment, economic resource management, and strategic town control rather than direct godlike intervention.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes emergent 'artificial life' simulation where NPCs have autonomous agency (fishing, farming, collecting wood) independent of player direction. Rejects micromanagement in favor of high-level strategic decisions. Command lag mechanic creates genuine logistical constraints—distance matters. Food is the binding resource constraint, not unit count.

difficulty.checkpointing: Per-map victory conditions; player must conquer most/all towns and eliminate opposing captains to win each map
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls army posture (passive/neutral/aggressive) affecting resource collection speed and combat behavior. Captain placement and preservation directly impacts army capacity. Food management creates natural difficulty scaling.

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment acquisition from towns (bows, swords, pikes prioritized by auto-distribution logic). Town specialization creates strategic hotspots—certain towns are sole/rare producers of useful items.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential map conquest; larger towns become accessible once smaller settlements establish military foundation. Neutral captain recruitment through town conquest expands command capacity.
replayability_hooks: Environmental storytelling through deforestation weather effects
Different equipment availability per map affects tactical approach
World War I expansion pack offering alternative setting with war machines

world_building_techniques: NPC emergent behavior (fishing, farming, shepherding visible on map)
Individual unit identity system (query tool shows name, sex, age, allegiance, hometown, vital stats)
Setting-based context (medieval conquest in base game; WWI in expansion)

boss_design.philosophy: Enemy captains serve as territorial commanders whose elimination is mandatory for map victory. Captains carry equipment and command armies. Neutral captains can be recruited if defeated without death, rewarding non-lethal tactical victory.
notable_bosses: Enemy Captains (map-specific commanders; defeating without killing allows recruitment)
Opposing armies defending towns (scale with town size)

faction_indicators: Allegiance tracking (NPC query shows allegiance status)
Town control indicators on balance-of-power scale
Captain command hierarchies (player character > subordinate captains > soldiers)
danger_signals: Large enemy armies clustering near player-controlled towns
Enemy captain survival and army reformation after defeat
Food supply depletion triggering starvation mechanics
Deforestation shifting weather toward hostile conditions
environmental_storytelling: Autonomous NPC labor visible (villages actively fishing, farming, collecting resources)
Seasonal resource variation (rain/snow patterns change based on environmental state)
Equipment manufacturing tied to specific town locations (scarcity creates narrative of resource specialization)
Wandering sheep herds as environmental feature and food source
